findings.finding: CORS allow_origins=['*'] allows any origin to make credentialed cross-origin requests to all API endpoints
findings.evidence:
app.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware, allow_origins=["*"], allow_methods=["*"],
                   allow_headers=["*"])
findings.remediation: Restrict allow_origins to the actual frontend origins (e.g. the same host, or a configurable whitelist). At minimum set allow_credentials=False explicitly and use a config-driven origin list.
findings.impact: Any malicious site can issue cross-origin requests to the PA API. Combined with bearer token in JS (localStorage), a CSRF-like attack can exfiltrate PII (chat logs, memories, persona profile).

findings.finding: POST /settings/llm accepts arbitrary api_key values over the network and stores them in process memory; the key is echoed back (masked) but the raw value persists for 4 hours
findings.remediation: Either disallow api_key changes via API (require env-only), or add re-authentication (require current token) before accepting api_key writes. Reduce TTL or encrypt at rest.
findings.impact: An attacker with a stolen bearer token can inject their own LLM API key, potentially redirecting all LLM calls to an attacker-controlled endpoint to harvest user conversations and PII.

findings.finding: POST /settings/llm allows changing base_url, enabling SSRF by redirecting LLM HTTP calls to arbitrary internal endpoints
findings.remediation: Validate base_url against an allowlist of known LLM provider URLs, or block RFC1918/loopback/link-local addresses. Better: don't allow base_url changes via API at all.
findings.impact: Attacker can set base_url to http://169.254.169.254/ (cloud metadata) or internal services, using the LLM HTTP client as an SSRF proxy.

findings.finding: Dev-mode fallback silently disables all authentication when PA_API_TOKEN is unset, making all endpoints (including PII data) publicly accessible
findings.remediation: Add a startup warning log + require an explicit PA_DEV_MODE=1 env var to enable open access. In production, refuse to start without PA_API_TOKEN set. Alternatively, bind to 127.0.0.1 only when no token is configured.
findings.impact: If deployed to a public-facing host without PA_API_TOKEN (easy misconfiguration), all user PII (conversations, memories, persona, calendar events) is exposed without any authentication.

findings.finding: Exempt prefix tuple includes /ws/ but WebSocket upgrade requests may not go through HTTP middleware at all; the exemption is redundant but creates confusion about the security boundary
findings.evidence: _EXEMPT_PREFIXES = ("/health", "/web", "/android", "/favicon", "/ws/")
findings.remediation: Remove /ws/ from _EXEMPT_PREFIXES since WS endpoints have their own auth (verify_ws_token). Document clearly that WS auth is handled separately.
findings.impact: No direct exploit, but maintenance confusion. If a future developer adds an HTTP endpoint under /ws/ prefix, it would be unintentionally exempted from auth.

findings.finding: Bing/Baidu HTML scraping uses a deceptive User-Agent string impersonating Chrome browser, violating search engine ToS
findings.evidence:
req = urllib.request.Request(
    url, headers={"User-Agent": "personal-assistant/0.3 (research bot; "
                               "Mozilla/5.0 Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) "
                               "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0 Safari/537.36"})
findings.remediation: Use a proper bot User-Agent (e.g. 'personal-assistant/0.3 (+https://github.com/...)') or switch to official search APIs (Bing Web Search API, Baidu API).
findings.impact: ToS violation risk; Bing/Baidu may block the IP or take legal action. The scraping also breaks when HTML structure changes.

findings.finding: ApiWebSearcher._generic() builds URL from user-controlled config (base_url) and issues HTTP requests without any URL validation, enabling SSRF if config is attacker-influenced
findings.evidence:
url = self.base_url + ("&" if "?" in self.base_url else "?") + urllib.parse.urlencode(params)
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "personal-assistant/0.3"})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=20) as r:
findings.remediation: Validate base_url scheme (https only) and hostname (not internal/private IP ranges) before making requests.
findings.impact: If an attacker can modify config (via /settings/llm or similar future endpoint), they can trigger SSRF to internal services.

findings.finding: POST /chat, POST /ingest, POST /distill, POST /triggers have no rate limiting; an attacker can trigger expensive LLM calls repeatedly
findings.remediation: Add per-IP or per-token rate limiting (e.g. slowapi or custom middleware). Critical for LLM-calling endpoints to prevent cost abuse.
findings.impact: Unbounded LLM API cost via automated rapid requests. Also potential DoS vector since LLM calls block thread pool workers.

findings.finding: System prompt includes full persona profile JSON and memory contents, which are sent verbatim to external LLM providers. User PII leaks to third-party LLM APIs.
findings.evidence:
prompt = (
    "[TASK:CHAT]\n"
    ...
    + json.dumps(profile, ensure_ascii=False)
    + "\n\n相关记忆：\n"
    + json.dumps(mem_snip, ensure_ascii=False)
findings.remediation: Document PII data flow to users clearly. Consider filtering/summarizing PII before sending to external LLM. For on-prem deployments (ollama), this is acceptable; for cloud LLMs, add a consent/disclosure mechanism.
findings.impact: All persona data (personality analysis, habits, goals, emotional states) and relevant memories are sent to external LLM providers on every chat request.

findings.finding: xiaozhi WebSocket endpoint bypasses the standard verify_ws_token() and implements its own custom auth logic, creating inconsistent security boundary
findings.remediation: Refactor to use auth.verify_ws_token() first, then fall back to header-based auth. Or extend verify_ws_token() to also check Authorization header.
findings.impact: Duplicated auth logic is harder to maintain and audit. If auth._check() is modified, developers might not realize xiaozhi_endpoint uses it directly.

findings.finding: Memory ID generation uses abs(hash()) which is non-deterministic across Python runs (PYTHONHASHSEED) and has collision risk due to modulo 10**12
findings.evidence: mid = mem.get("id") or f"m-{abs(hash((mem.get('segment_id',''), mem.get('content','')[:40])))%10**12}"
findings.remediation: Use uuid4() or a deterministic hash like hashlib.sha256() for ID generation. Same issue in add_event (line 219), add_reminder (line 254), add_intervention (line 184).
findings.impact: ID collisions cause silent data overwrites (INSERT OR REPLACE). Non-deterministic IDs break reproducibility across process restarts.

findings.finding: CONFIG is loaded at module import time as a module-level global, meaning .env changes after startup are never picked up, and config reload requires process restart
findings.evidence: CONFIG = load_config()
findings.remediation: This is acceptable for a single-process app. However, the runtime override layer (set_override) partially addresses this. Document that env changes require restart.
findings.impact: Low operational impact but can cause confusion when debugging config issues.

findings.finding: broadcast() iterates over active connections without holding the lock, causing potential ConcurrentModificationError if connect/disconnect happens simultaneously
findings.remediation: Acquire self._lock before iterating, or use a snapshot under lock: `async with self._lock: snapshot = list(self.active)` then iterate snapshot outside lock.
findings.impact: Race condition can cause missed broadcasts or exceptions when a WebSocket is added/removed during iteration. list() copy mitigates but doesn't fully solve.

findings.finding: API keys are sent in HTTP headers over urllib without certificate verification enforcement (urllib uses system CA bundle but doesn't pin certificates)
findings.remediation: This is acceptable for standard HTTPS. However, if base_url is changed to HTTP (no TLS), api_key is sent in cleartext. Add a check that base_url uses https:// for non-localhost URLs.
findings.impact: If base_url is set to http:// (non-TLS), API keys are transmitted in cleartext. Combined with H-003 (base_url changeable via API), this is exploitable.

findings.finding: DELETE /chat-log has no confirmation or undo mechanism; a single unauthenticated (dev mode) or token-bearing request permanently deletes all chat history
findings.remediation: Add a soft-delete mechanism or require a confirmation parameter. Consider adding an admin-only role for destructive operations.
findings.impact: Accidental or malicious deletion of entire chat history with no recovery path (no backup/undo).

findings.finding: scan_once() and run_loop() methods are defined inside query_habits() function body due to incorrect indentation, making them unreachable dead code
findings.remediation: These methods were likely intended to be part of IngestionPipeline class. Move them to the correct indentation level inside the class body.
findings.impact: Dead code that cannot be reached. If IngestionPipeline.scan_once() or .run_loop() is called elsewhere, it will raise AttributeError.

findings.finding: CLINotifier writes to a log file without any rotation or size limit, which can grow unbounded on long-running instances
findings.evidence:
def notify(self, message: str, evidence: str, trigger_kind: str):
    line = f"[{storage.now_iso()}] ({trigger_kind}) {message}  ← {evidence}"
    ...
    with self.log_path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(line + "\n")
findings.remediation: Use logging module with RotatingFileHandler, or implement a size check and rotation.
findings.impact: Disk space exhaustion on long-running deployments.

findings.finding: Every function call to connect() re-executes the full SCHEMA DDL (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS x11 + CREATE INDEX), adding overhead to every database operation
findings.evidence:
def connect(db_path: Path | None = None):
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path or config.sqlite_path())
    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    conn.executescript(SCHEMA)
    return conn
findings.remediation: Cache a flag after first schema creation, or check for table existence before running DDL. Alternatively, use a connection pool.
findings.impact: Minor performance overhead on every DB call. No correctness issue due to IF NOT EXISTS.

findings.finding: cmd_serve binds to 0.0.0.0 by default, exposing the server on all network interfaces including public ones
findings.evidence: s = sub.add_parser("serve"); s.add_argument("--host", default="0.0.0.0"); s.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8000)
findings.remediation: Default to 127.0.0.1 for safety. Require explicit --host 0.0.0.0 for network-accessible deployment.
findings.impact: Combined with H-004 (no auth in dev mode), the server is publicly accessible with all PII exposed when run without PA_API_TOKEN.

findings.finding: All memory contents (potentially large PII corpus) are serialized as JSON and sent to LLM in a single request with no size cap
findings.evidence:
mem_json = [{"kind": m.get("kind"), "content": m.get("content"),
             "evidence": m.get("evidence")} for m in mems]
user = ("Current profile (JSON or null):\n" + json.dumps(cur, ensure_ascii=False)
        + "\nRecent memories (JSON):\n" + json.dumps(mem_json, ensure_ascii=False))
findings.remediation: Add a cap on the number of memories sent (e.g. most recent 100) or batch distillation across multiple LLM calls. Check total token count before sending.
findings.impact: With large memory stores, the request may exceed LLM context window limits causing errors, or incur excessive API costs.

findings.finding: Config file path is hardcoded to config/default.json with no environment variable override for custom config paths
findings.evidence: CONFIG_PATH = ROOT / "config" / "default.json"
findings.remediation: Add PA_CONFIG_PATH env var support: CONFIG_PATH = Path(os.environ.get('PA_CONFIG_PATH', ROOT / 'config' / 'default.json'))
findings.impact: Limits deployment flexibility; multiple instances cannot use different config files without modifying source.

findings.finding: verify.py has good anti-hallucination design: deterministic time resolution, source-grounding checks, and automatic deletion of ungrounded data
findings.remediation: N/A - positive finding. The anti-hallucination verification pipeline is well-designed.
findings.impact: Positive: prevents LLM-hallucinated dates from persisting in the calendar/reminder system.

findings.finding: Temporary WAV files are written to data/inbox/archive/ and unlinked in finally block, but unlink failure is silently caught
findings.evidence:
fd, path = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=".wav", dir=str(archive))
...
finally:
    try:
        os.unlink(path)
    except OSError:
        pass
findings.remediation: Log the unlink failure for debugging. Consider using tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile with delete=True instead.
findings.impact: Orphaned temporary WAV files may accumulate on disk if unlink fails repeatedly.
